description: Strong mean-reverting regime: returns strictly alternate sign, producing strongly negative lag-1 autocorrelation. Vol is moderate. Expected label: 'mean-reverting'.

inputs.description: 20-day perfectly alternating +0.02/-0.02 series. Lag-1 AC = -1.0 (population) by construction: every up day is followed by a down day and vice versa. Rolling 20-day std = 0.02 (moderate vol).

derivation.lag1_ac: For x_t = +A on even, -A on odd indices: cov(x_t, x_{t-1}) = E[(+A)(-A)] = -A^2; var(x_t) = A^2. So rho = -1.0 (population). The sample estimate with N=20 will be close to -1.0 but not exact due to endpoint effects; any correct AC computation will yield a strongly negative value, well below any 'trending' threshold.
derivation.realized_vol: std(series) = sqrt(sum((x_t - mean)^2) / (N-1)) with mean=0. All 20 values are +-0.02. Sample var = (20 * 0.0004) / 19 = 0.008/19 = 0.000421... Vol = sqrt(0.000421) ≈ 0.02052. This is a mid-range vol, well within 'normal' vol territory, not 'high-vol'.
derivation.expected_label: Lag-1 AC strongly negative (-1.0 population) -> mean-reverting regardless of vol tercile assignment, because the AC signal is the primary discriminator for mean-reverting vs trending.
derivation.tolerance_note: Expected label is a string, compared exactly.
